agent_roles.research_agent: Pre-Round Research + four-round dossier per subtopic

preamble.objective: Completar PHASE 2 (investigación pre-sección) de S02: research dossier de 8 subtemas (4×2) listo para PHASE 3 blueprint.
scope_in: Mapeo de 8 subtemas a slugs de examen y gaps
Decisiones técnicas (Decimal vs float, is vs ==, f-strings, preserve raw)
preamble.stop_criterion: Cada uno de los 8 subtemas tiene: fuentes primarias, benchmark curricular, patrones prácticos, pedagogía, riesgos y notas de autoría.

rounds.name: Documentación oficial actualizada
findings: docs.python.org/3/tutorial/introduction.html (Python 3.14.6, updated Jul 20 2026): int/float, /, //, %, **, assignment, str immutability, list mutability, simple assignment never copies, slice shallow copy, print().
docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html: truth values, comparisons including is/is not, numeric ops, constructor conversion notes.
docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html: Decimal for money; construct from strings not floats; quantize for fixed places; ROUND_HALF_EVEN default; FloatOperation trap.
docs.python.org/3/reference/lexical_analysis.html#f-strings: f-strings PEP 498/701; format specs; debug =; nested braces; t-strings new in 3.14 (mention only, not required S02).
peps.python.org/pep-0008/: snake_case functions/vars; UPPER_CASE constants; CapWords classes; avoid l/O/I single letters.

rounds.name: Repositorios y patrones prácticos 2025-2026
findings: Patrón intake: dict con campos *_raw + campos normalizados; never overwrite raw on parse fail.
Validación: try int()/Decimal() + mensajes que digan qué campo falló y valor recibido.
Unicode: str is Unicode by default; names with tildes (García, Ñahui) must round-trip.
Pyodide/browser: decimal stdlib available; input() may need simulated inputs in playground.

rounds.name: Tutoriales y master classes (benchmark, no copiar)
findings: CS50P Lectures on types, variables, input — sequence benchmark only.
Python Tutorial ch.3 Informal Introduction — canonical first-hours path.
Real Python / official only as secondary; primary always docs.python.org.

rounds.name: Patrones pedagógicos cursos Python/DS
findings: Andamiaje: literales → type inspect → assign names → operators → I/O parse.
Errores novato: = vs ==; float money; mutar lista vía alias; int(' 12 ') vs int('12.5'); perder raw al normalizar.
I Do en browser/local pure Python; demos con expected output comprobables.

deprecation_check.percent_formatting: Still valid but f-strings preferred for new S02 content
deprecation_check.t_strings: Python 3.14 feature — optional note only, not assessment target

subtopics.title: Identidad, mutabilidad y copias
subtopics.v3_text: identidad, mutabilidad y copias al nivel necesario

subtopics.title: Parsing, mensajes de error y ejemplos comprobables
subtopics.v3_text: parsing, mensajes de error y ejemplos comprobables
subtopics.exam_skill: parsear campos y reportar errores accionables

S02-T1-A.title: Literales y tipos

round_1_primary.source: Python Tutorial — An Informal Introduction to Python
key_facts: int vs float; / always float; // floor; % remainder; ** power
list mutable; simple assignment does not copy
bool is subtype of int (teach carefully, not abuse)
String prefixes r/f/b; t-strings 3.14 optional mention

round_2_curricular.use: Benchmark secuencia tipos antes de control; no copiar problem sets

round_3_practical.pattern: Tabla de literales: 42, 3.14, 'hola', True, None con type()
round_3_practical.anti_pattern: Enseñar set/dict/tuple depth en S02 (solo mención si hace falta para mutabilidad)

round_4_pedagogy: Demo: imprimir type de 6 literales de intake (nombre str, edad int, activo bool)
E1 guiado literales; E2 clasificar tipo de expresión; E3 detectar literal vs string numérico '42'
authoring_notes: No listar todos los dunder types; focus student-facing int/float/str/bool/None
risks: No saturar con complex/bytes en S02

S02-T1-B.title: Inspección, conversión y validación explícita

round_1_primary.source: Built-in Functions — type, isinstance, int, float, str
key_facts: isinstance preferred over type(x) is T for inheritance
int('42') ok; int('3.14') ValueError; float('3.14') ok
Object type comparisons should use isinstance()

round_2_curricular: Validación explícita: convertir → capturar ValueError → mensaje con campo
round_3_practical: strip() before int on user text
Empty string is not a valid int — explicit check
Preserve original string when conversion fails
round_4_pedagogy: Demo: convertir ' 19 ' a int; fallar 'diecinueve' con mensaje
E3 transfer: validar teléfono como dígitos sin afirmar que es persona real
authoring_notes: Link to T4-B for full parse pipeline

S02-T2-A.title: Asignación y convenciones

key_facts: = binds name to object; no type declaration
avoid l, O, I single-letter names

round_2_curricular: Contrast = assignment vs == comparison early (exam concept reuse)
round_3_practical: Names for intake: nombres, apellido_paterno, apellido_materno, telefono, direccion
Trailing underscore if clash with keyword (class_)
round_4_pedagogy: E1 rename camelCase to snake_case; E2 fix illegal names; E3 style review mini-diff
authoring_notes: No force Spanish identifiers in code — Spanish field names OK as domain
risks: No confuse students with full PEP 8 document — extract essentials

S02-T2-B.title: Identidad, mutabilidad y copias

round_1_primary.source: Built-in Types — Comparisons is / is not
copy — Shallow and deep copy operations
key_facts: S02 level: list[:] or list.copy() enough
Deep copy only mention for nested later

round_2_curricular: Preserve original intake fields: store raw string separately before normalize
round_3_practical: Demo: a = b = []; a.append(1); see b
Demo: raw = nombre; nombre = nombre.strip().title(); raw unchanged if different binding
round_4_pedagogy: E1 predict id/is outcomes; E2 fix accidental alias bug; E3 design record with raw + clean
authoring_notes: Keep shallow only; no deep copy rabbit hole
Link gate: 'sin perder el valor original'
risks: Interning of small ints/strings — avoid relying on is for values

S02-T3-A.title: Aritmética, comparación y precedencia
round_3_practical: Age range checks later use comparisons; S02 stays expression-level
Do not teach full if/elif trees here (S03)
round_4_pedagogy: E3: fix precedence bug in monoto total = precio + precio * igv
authoring_notes: Mention = vs == as bridge from T2-A
risks: Don't import bitwise as primary S02 content

S02-T3-B.title: Dinero, Decimal, precisión y redondeo

round_1_primary.source: decimal — Decimal fixed-point and floating-point arithmetic
key_facts: 0.1 + 0.1 + 0.1 - 0.3 exact zero with Decimal; not with float

round_2_curricular: Accounting/fintech preference for decimal arithmetic in Python stdlib
round_3_practical: Never sum floats for money in recommended path
str to Decimal for user input amounts
round_4_pedagogy: E2: total con IGV 18% en Decimal; E3: quantize report
authoring_notes: getcontext().prec only if needed; quantize more important for money display
risks: Mixing Decimal and float raises TypeError in arithmetic
Don't require full context trap mastery

S02-T4-A.title: input, print y f-strings

key_facts: Always str from input — need conversion

round_3_practical: Playground: simulate inputs as variables if input() awkward
authoring_notes: t-strings 3.14: optional 'existe en 3.14' one-liner not assessed
risks: Security: don't teach that input is safe for eval/SQL

S02-T4-B.title: Parsing, mensajes de error y ejemplos comprobables

key_facts: ValueError for right type wrong value (int('abc'))
Messages should be actionable for operators
str holds Unicode; source UTF-8 default
Names with tildes/ñ must not be stripped incorrectly

round_2_curricular: Test table: valid, empty, Unicode name, invalid number — gate of S02
round_3_practical: Empty field → error 'campo X vacío' without dropping raw
Invalid number field (e.g. edad or telefono digits) → keep raw, append error
round_4_pedagogy: I Do: parse one synthetic client line or prompts
E1 guided empty; E2 Unicode García Ñahui; E3 invalid number + assert tests
authoring_notes: Tests comprobables: functions return structured result for assert
risks: Don't overbuild full validation engine (that's S03 rules)

source_registry.title: PEP 8 — Style Guide for Python Code
CS50's Introduction to Programming with Python

decisions_for_blueprint.decision: Estructura canónica 4×2 con slugs: literals-types, type-inspect-convert, assignment-naming, identity-mutability, operators-precedence, decimal-money, io-fstrings, parse-error-messages
Rescope platform content away from conditionals/loops/functions/comprehensions as S02 required outcomes; those remain S03/S04
Money examples always Decimal constructed from str; float only for counter-example
Project increment = intake parser with raw preservation + tests empty/Unicode/invalid number
Environment browser-pyodide and local-python; pure stdlib
Shallow copy only (list[:] / .copy()); deep copy out of scope
No copiar ejercicios de CS50P; only sequence patterns
Title decision: keep shortTitle 'Basics de Python'; retarget tagline to V3 scope (valores, tipos, operadores e I/O)
decisions_for_blueprint.authority: Platform id basics + V3 title phrase

misconceptions_to_address: Usar float para dinero en soles
Creer que a = b copia una lista
Usar is para comparar valores numéricos/strings (salvo None)
Normalizar y sobrescribir el valor original del cliente
Tratar string vacío como 0 sin validación explícita
Asumir que nombres con tildes fallan en Python str
